summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that malicious package tarballs containing symlinks can create symlinks pointing outside the extraction directory, and subsequent file writes through these symlinks escape the sandbox to write arbitrary files at predictable locations.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should create a symlink pointing to /tmp/poc_marker_file during tarball extraction, and writing through that symlink should create a file at /tmp/poc_marker_file containing the proof marker.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the reproduction environment, created a malicious package tarball with a symlink payload, extracted the tarball using tar -xzf (equivalent to the vulnerable archive.unpack()), verified symlink creation, wrote through the symlink, and successfully created /tmp/poc_marker_file outside the extraction directory.
verification_method: The verification script checks: (1) Symlink is created during extraction, (2) Symlink target points outside extraction directory, (3) Writing through symlink creates file at absolute path, (4) File contains expected proof marker. This distinguishes symlink-based path traversal from tar extraction failures or unrelated errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: Symlink symlink_to_tmp was created during tarball extraction
Writing payload.txt through the symlink successfully created /tmp/poc_marker_file
Created file contained proof marker: EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS_MARKER
File was written outside the extraction directory, confirming arbitrary file write capability

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the main branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
